creator: david@catwhisker.org
text:
Created attachment 192702
gzipped copy of the poudriere build log for lang/v8

I use portmaster for updating ports in my development systems, and poudriere to build local packages (on one of the development systems) for my "production" systems.

For my laptop (one of the development systems), I have lang/v8 built (as a build requirement for databases/mongodb); it was built & installed Mon Feb 19 03:53:13 2018 PST.  At that time, the laptop had been running

FreeBSD 11.1-STABLE #543  r329560M/329561:1101510: Mon Feb 19 03:41:52 PST 2018     root@g1-215.catwhisker.org:/common/S1/obj/usr/src/sys/CANARY  amd64

and I have had no need to update the port (on the laptop) since.


On the machine where I run poudriere, it is currently running

FreeBSD freebeast.catwhisker.org 11.2-PRERELEASE FreeBSD 11.2-PRERELEASE #664  r332855M/332855:1101515: Sat Apr 21 03:44:37 PDT 2018     root@freebeast.catwhisker.org:/common/S1/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64

(which is also what was copied to the build jail).

As I update the production machines on Sunday morning, I do a "catch-up" poudriere run Saturday morning (today); it is that run that (again) complained about lang/v8 failing to build.  I will upload the (gzipped) build log.

As there have been some recent updates to clang in FreeBSD base (stable/11), I suspect that lang/v8 may be running afoul of tighter checking, or some such thing.
